zh.lifeSummary: 當Gisela von Berg於1096年出生在德國海因里希_普洛文次 維斯特法倫阿爾騰亞賀爾謝易德伯格時，她的父親Adolf II. von Berg-Hövel51歲，母親Adelheid von Laufen47歲。她於1116年與Graf Sizzo III von Schwarzburg結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1143年3月20日之後。

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Berg
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Lennart, Bjorn, Lars, Nils, Alf, Nels, Thor, Tor, Knute, Selmer, Anders.

German and Dutch: topographic name for someone who lived on or by a hill or mountain, from Middle High German berc, Middle Dutch berghe. This surname is also found in some other parts of Europe, e.g. in France (Alsace and Lorraine) and Poland. Compare Dutch Vandenberg .
Norwegian and Danish: habitational name from a farmstead named with Old Norse bjarg ‘mountain, hill’.

variants.name: Berger
meanings: German, Dutch, Swedish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): topographic name for someone who lived in the mountains or hills (see   Berg  ). The surname of German origin is also found in many other European countries, e.g. in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Russia, Poland, Czechia, Hungary, and Croatia, and Slovenia, often as a translation into German of corresponding Slavic topographic names or surnames. As a Jewish name it is mainly artificial. Compare   Bargar  ,   Barger  ,   Barker  ,   Barrier  , and   Barriger  .
French: occupational name from Old French bergier ‘shepherd’ (from Late Latin berbicarius, from berbex ‘ram’). It is also found in England, as a surname of Huguenot origin. Compare Shepard .
Norwegian: habitational name from any of various farms so named with the plural of   Berg   ‘mountain’.

variants.name: Bergh
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Johan, Anders, Arndt, Owe, Per, Sigvard, Sven, Syvert.
Scandinavian: variant of   Berg  .
Dutch and English: topographic name denoting someone living on or by a hill, Middle Dutch berghe, Middle English bergh ‘hill’ (Old English beorg). Compare Dutch Berg and Vandenbergh .

variants.name: Vandenberg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Dirk, Marinus, Gerrit, Gerritt, Hendrik, Jacobus, Maarten, Pieter, Wim, Albertus, Durk, Hendrick.
Dutch (Van den Berg) and Flemish (also Van den Berg): topographic name for someone who lived on or by a hill, Middle Dutch berghe. Compare Berg .

variants.name: Berglin
meanings: Swedish: variant of Berg + the suffix -lin, derived from Latin -(l)inius ‘relating to’.

variants.name: Berget
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Knut.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of several farm names especially in eastern Norway named with the definite singular form of berg ‘mountain, hill’ (see Berg ).
French: topographic name, a derivative of   Berge   ‘river bank’.

variants.name: Baerg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Kurt.
German (Bärg): variant of Berg .

variants.name: Bergen
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from any of various places called Bergen.
Dutch and Flemish: topographic name for someone who lived on or by a hill (see   Berg  ), or a habitational name from any of various places so named (see   Van Bergen  ).
Swedish (mainly Bergén): ornamental or habitational name formed with the (placename) element berg ‘mountain, hill’ + the adjectival suffix -en/-én, a derivative of Latin -enius ‘relating to’.

variants.name: Berghorst
meanings: Dutch (Gelderland): topographic name for someone living on or by a hill with shrubberies (see   Berg   and   Horst  ).

variants.name: Barg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Bernhard, Erwin, Hans, Mathias, Wilfried.
North German:
topographic name for someone who lived on a hill, Low German barg. Compare Berg .

variants.name: Berge
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Maren, Johan, Karsten, Alf, Bent, Erik, Gudmund, Knud, Peer, Per, Sten, Thor.
Norwegian and Swedish: variant of   Berg  , from the dative form. As a Norwegian surname, this is a habitational name from any of numerous farms so named all over the country.
German: topographic name for a person by or on a hill, from an extended form of Berg (formerly with a preposition with dative case, e.g. am Berge).

nameMeanings.name: Gisela
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from gisil ‘pledge’ or ‘scion’. See Giselle .

variants.name: Giselle
meanings: name of Frankish origin, from the Germanic word gisil ‘pledge’. It was a common practice in medieval Europe to leave children as pledges for an alliance, to be brought up at a foreign court, and the name may be derived as a byname from this practice. This was the name by which the wife of Duke Rollo of Normandy ( c.860– c.930 ) was known. On her account the name enjoyed considerable popularity in France from an early period. Use of the name in English-speaking countries is much more recent, and is due mainly to the ballet Giselle (first performed in 1841 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Gisela von Berg was born in 1096, in Berg, Herscheid, Altena, Westphalia, Prussia, Germany, her father, Adolf II. von Berg-Hövel, was 51 and her mother, Adelheid von Laufen, was 47. She married Graf Sizzo III von Schwarzburg in 1116. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. She died after 20 March 1143.

Elle a épousé Graf Sizzo III von Schwarzburg en 1116. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée après 20 mars 1143.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Gisela von Berg è nata il nel 1096 nel Berg, Herscheid, Altena, Provinz Westfalen, Preußen, Germania, sua padre, Adolf II. von Berg-Hövel, aveva 51 anni e sua madre, Adelheid von Laufen, aveva 47 anni. Lei sposato Graf Sizzo III von Schwarzburg nel 1116. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie. Morì dopo il 20 marzo 1143.

ko.lifeSummary: Gisela von Berg(이)가 1096년 독일 플레우ss 플로빈츠 웨스트파렌 알텐아 허슈이드 버그에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Adolf II. von Berg-Hövel(은)는 51세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Adelheid von Laufen(은)는 47세였다. 그녀는 1116년 Graf Sizzo III von Schwarzburg(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1143년 3월 20일 이후 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Gisela von Berg родилась в 1096 г. в местности бирг, хирщид, алтена, провинз вестфеилен, Пруссия, Германия, её отцу по имени Adolf II. von Berg-Hövel было 51 год, а матери, которую звали Adelheid von Laufen, 47. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Graf Sizzo III von Schwarzburg в 1116 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла после 20 марта 1143 г.
